events.desc: First known suspected case — a health worker — reports onset of symptoms including fever, hemorrhaging, vomiting and intense malaise. The case died at a medical centre in Bunia.
WHO alerted of a high-mortality outbreak of unknown illness in Mongbwalu Health Zone, Ituri Province, DRC. Four health workers died within 4 days.
Rapid response team investigation in Mongbwalu and Rwampara health zones confirms Bundibugyo virus disease (BVD) caused by Bundibugyo virus (BDBV).
INRB Kinshasa analyzed 13 blood samples from Rwampara Health Zone, Ituri Province.

Laboratory analysis confirmed BVD in 8 of 13 samples. DRC Ministry of Health officially declares the 17th Ebola outbreak. 246 suspected cases and 80 deaths across Rwampara, Mongbwalu, and Bunia. Uganda confirms first imported case — an elderly man admitted May 11, died May 14.

WHO Director-General declares a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) under IHR. Uganda confirms 2nd imported case from DRC. No licensed vaccine or therapeutics exist for Bundibugyo virus.
1 American healthcare worker exposed in DRC tests positive and is medically evacuated to Germany for treatment. 6 high-risk contacts also transferred to Germany for monitoring.
US CDC and DHS implement enhanced travel screening and entry restrictions. Africa CDC declares Public Health Emergency of Continental Security.
Outbreak spreads to Nord-Kivu Province — cases reported in Goma. Over 1,000 contacts under follow-up in Ituri.
Outbreak expands to 3rd province — Sud-Kivu confirmed in a person who travelled from Tsopo Province. Uganda announces 3 additional cases, all linked to previously confirmed DRC travellers.

DRC Ministry of Health reports 904 suspected cases, 101 confirmed, 119 suspected deaths, 10 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, and Sud-Kivu provinces. Uganda: 5 confirmed cases, 1 confirmed death — all linked to DRC travel, no local spread.

DRC reports 906 suspected cases (223 deaths) and 105 confirmed cases (10 deaths). Uganda: 7 confirmed cases, 1 death. Two suspected cases reported in Italy — travellers from Uganda hospitalised in isolation in Milan, Lombardy. Italy under investigation.

nodes.label: Rwampara HZ
nodes.detail: 6 health areas affected · confirmed outbreak origin · per-zone breakdown not reported by CDC

nodes.label: Mongbwalu HZ
nodes.detail: 3 health areas affected · 4 HCWs dead by May 9 · per-zone breakdown not reported by CDC

nodes.label: Bunia HZ
nodes.detail: Epicentre hub · 906 suspected cases · 223 suspected deaths · 3 provinces confirmed

nodes.label: Kampala, Uganda
nodes.detail: 9 confirmed cases · 2 confirmed death · Kampala · 5 linked to first 2 cases

nodes.label: Milan, Italy
nodes.detail: 2 suspected cases · travellers from Uganda · hospitalised in isolation in Milan · May 25 · under investigation

nodes.label: Germany
nodes.detail: 1 American HCW (confirmed) + 6 high-risk contacts evacuated · May 17 · no local transmission

nodes.label: United States
nodes.detail: Enhanced screening at entry points · May 18 · no confirmed cases · risk assessed as low
